mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS levels against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply expansion, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO growth rates yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression predicting 20-day returns over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes where CPI exceeds 235, rapid monetary tightening forces a repricing of equity risk premiums, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235, or the conditional predictive accuracy drops below 45% in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: Higher nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer expenditure and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS levels and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows, or the signal reverses direction consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1100 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and household wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where HOUST drops below 1100, or the threshold effect vanishes in a quantile regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production combined with rising inflation indicates cost-push supply shocks that erode corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of low INDPRO growth and high CPIAUCSL growth fails to predict negative returns, with a classification accuracy below 48% in a logistic regression over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.0 percent, aggregate demand weakness forces earnings downgrades across cyclical sectors, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.0%, or the predictive power disappears after controlling for monetary policy shifts.
